_**Agent (default)**_

Documentation is in `docs/features/` with Mermaid diagrams for each call flow.

## What was documented

The codebase currently has one bounded context — **Registration** — with five vertical slices. Each slice has its own doc with sequence diagrams, call chains, validation rules, and error responses.

| Slice | Documentation |
|-------|---------------|
| List cases | [docs/features/registration/list-registration-cases.md](docs/features/registration/list-registration-cases.md) |
| Open case | [docs/features/registration/open-registration-case.md](docs/features/registration/open-registration-case.md) |
| Get case detail | [docs/features/registration/get-registration-case.md](docs/features/registration/get-registration-case.md) |
| Record identity | [docs/features/registration/record-identity.md](docs/features/registration/record-identity.md) |
| Attach document | [docs/features/registration/attach-document.md](docs/features/registration/attach-document.md) |

## Index and overview

- **[docs/features/README.md](docs/features/README.md)** — shared architecture diagram (Blazor vs API → handler → domain → infrastructure) and end-to-end user journey
- **[docs/features/registration/README.md](docs/features/registration/README.md)** — domain model ER diagram and slice index

## Diagram types used

Each feature doc includes:

1. **Sequence diagram** — full call flow for both Blazor (direct handler injection) and HTTP API paths
2. **Call chain** — text tree from UI component to persistence
3. **State diagram** (where relevant) — e.g. identity recording on `RecordIdentity`

The shared pattern across all features:

```
Blazor page/component ──direct──► Handler ──► Domain aggregate ──► Repository ──► DB
HTTP client ──► Endpoint (validation + status mapping) ──► Handler ──► ...
```

When new bounded contexts are added under `Web/Features/`, the same structure can be reused: one folder per context, one markdown file per slice.

there is an error when I load the  @docs/features/registration/README.md  I got this error: 

Unable to render rich display

Parse error on line 3:
...egistrationCase ||--{ AdministrativeDocu
-----------------------^
Expecting 'ZERO_OR_ONE', 'ZERO_OR_MORE', 'ONE_OR_MORE', 'ONLY_ONE', 'MD_PARENT', got 'BLOCK_START'

For more information, see https://docs.github.com/get-started/writing-on-github/working-with-advanced-formatting/creating-diagrams#creating-mermaid-diagrams

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user encountered a Mermaid parse error while loading the ER diagram in docs/features/registration/README.md.

The error stems from invalid Mermaid ER diagram syntax. The `||--{` relationship notation appears incorrect. Valid Mermaid cardinality symbols include `|o`, `||`, `}o`, and `}|`. The `{` character may also be misinterpreted as a block start.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Fixing the Mermaid ER diagram syntax — the relationship notation is invalid.


_**Agent (default)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="read_file_v2"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **read_file_v2** • Read file: /Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/docs/features/registration/README.md</summary>

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (default)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="edit_file_v2"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **edit_file_v2** • Edit file: /Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/docs/features/registration/README.md</summary>

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (default)**_

The ER diagram used invalid Mermaid relationship syntax. In Mermaid, cardinality must be written with full tokens on both sides — `||--{` is not valid because `{` by itself is parsed as a block start.

It’s now:

```mermaid
RegistrationCase ||--o{ AdministrativeDocument : "RegistrationCaseId"
```

That means one case has zero or many documents (`o{`), which matches the domain model. Reload the page and the diagram should render.
